name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Day 63 Thursday — ₹94.82 CLOSE (Apr 29; +0.2% rupee weaker, Day 3 of ₹94.50 breach); ₹95.00 TRIGGER ₹0.18 AWAY; FOMC HAWKISH-SKEWED DISSENT REMOVES THE DXY-RELIEF VECTOR YESTERDAY'S BRIEF FLAGGED. USD/INR closed Wed Apr 29 at ₹94.73 (-0.2% rupee weaker), intraday low ₹94.80 in early trade before RBI selective dollar-sales pared losses (CONFIRMED — Business Standard, TradingEconomics, MultiBagg). Day 2 of post-₹94.50-breach regime — breach held, rupee did NOT snap back with RBI intervention. ₹95.00 round-number trigger now ₹0.27 above spot. All-time low remains ₹95.21 from late-March; rupee fallen ~1.7% over past 7 sessions. Business Standard: 'RBI intervention cushions rupee as oil strain brings record low into sight.' RBI stance: SLOW-THE-DECLINE, NOT defend-fixed-level — selective dollar sales for vol-limit, NOT structural-floor defense. PARALLEL EM-ASIA SIGNAL: Indonesian rupiah record low Wed = broad oil-sensitive-EM-Asia stress NOT idiosyncratic India. Brent breached $115 intraday Wed (close $114.64 +3.04%) — every $1/bbl Brent = ~$1.5B India annual import bill; structural pressure intensifying. UAE OPEC exit Tue Apr 28 bullish-for-India medium-term but does NOT change today's import-bill arithmetic. RBI OMC special-credit-facility (IOC/HPCL/BPCL dollar-rationing) at Day 16-17 of 30-day forced-choice clock. Cumulative April RBI dollar sales $3.6B+. FX reserves ~$700.9B (Apr 10 print). FII April MTD selling ₹56,364 Cr (heavy). [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

current.regime: Day 5 of consecutive declines, -1.3% on week. RBI selling dollars intermittently to slow slide, not defending fixed level. ₹94.50 trigger remains WATCHING — not breached. Strength is RBI-engineered...
